applicabilityDetail.description: All students

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

CF 5140A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 5141A under a shared description block.
CF 5140A.description: An introduction to handling emergencies in the cockpit of an aircraft. Students will develop an appreciation for the complexities and additional burdens levied on aircrew during an emergency and understand how to properly assist during one.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, FTE, and STC students

CF 5141A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 5140A under a shared description block.
CF 5141A.description: An introduction to handling emergencies in the cockpit of an aircraft. Students will develop an appreciation for the complexities and additional burdens levied on aircrew during an emergency and understand how to properly assist during one.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

CF 5201A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 5210A and CF 5220A under a shared description block.
CF 5201A.description: Flight records briefing and in-processing. Students are introduced to safety privilege as well as safety reporting and mishap requirements. Training on local area procedures.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

CF 5210A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 5201A and CF 5220A under a shared description block.
CF 5210A.description: Flight records briefing and in-processing. Students are introduced to safety privilege as well as safety reporting and mishap requirements. Training on local area procedures.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

CF 5230A.description: Issue and training for electronic flight bags (EFB). Time allocated at the end of the TPS to turn in individually issued EFBs.

applicabilityDetail.description: FTC — all standard (fixed-wing) students

CF 5251H.description: Classroom and planeside instruction on the operation of the Data Acquisition Systems (DAS) installed in each curriculum aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

CF 5301A.description: This block is required by AFI and prepares all students for safe and effective operation as a flight crew or test team, including in the control room.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, FTE, and RPA students

CF 6101A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6102A and CF 6103G under a shared description block.
CF 6101A.description: The airmanship lecture introduces and reinforces basic aircraft concepts to students with limited aviation experience. The purpose is to build basic airmanship knowledge and skills required for students at TPS.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM and FTE students

CF 6102A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6101A and CF 6103G under a shared description block.
CF 6102A.description: The airmanship lecture introduces and reinforces basic aircraft concepts to students with limited aviation experience. The purpose is to build basic airmanship knowledge and skills required for students at TPS.

CF 6103G.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6101A and CF 6102A under a shared description block.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM and FTE students — C-172 airmanship flight 1 of 5

CF 6110F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6110-4F C-172 Airmanship FA-1-5 (ABM/FTE)'.
CF 6110F.description: The profiles for the flights are structured to start with the most basic aviation skills and subsequently build in complexity. The purpose of these hours is to expose the student to various aviation concepts common to ordinary flight. There is no requirement for these students to demonstrate solo proficiency in any maneuver.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM and FTE students — C-172 airmanship flight 2 of 5

CF 6111F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6110-4F C-172 Airmanship FA-1-5 (ABM/FTE)'.
CF 6111F.description: The profiles for the flights are structured to start with the most basic aviation skills and subsequently build in complexity. The purpose of these hours is to expose the student to various aviation concepts common to ordinary flight. There is no requirement for these students to demonstrate solo proficiency in any maneuver.

applicabilityDetail.description: CSO students only — C-172 airmanship flight 1 of 2

CF 6120F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6120-1F C-172 Airmanship CA-1-2 (CSO)'. MCG description references 'CF 6110-14F' but source series was 'CF 6110-4F'; preserved verbatim in description.
CF 6120F.description: Due to their previous aviation experience, CSO students will only receive 1-2 airmanship flights that consist of a compilation of the contact and instrument tasks from CF 6110-14F.

applicabilityDetail.description: CSO students only — C-172 airmanship flight 2 of 2

CF 6121F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6120-1F C-172 Airmanship CA-1-2 (CSO)'.
CF 6121F.description: Due to their previous aviation experience, CSO students will only receive 1-2 airmanship flights that consist of a compilation of the contact and instrument tasks from CF 6110-14F.

CF 6130A.description: This block covers a basic overview of the aircraft familiarization flights each ABM, CSO, FTE, and RPA student will have in the C-12, T-38, and F-16.

applicabilityDetail.description: FTC — all standard (fixed-wing) students

CF 6201M.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6202-3F under a shared description block.
CF 6201M.description: The purpose of the glider familiarization flights is to become familiar with glider (high L/D) flying while observing and practicing energy management. All students will receive at least two glider flights.

CF 6203F.description: The purpose of the glider familiarization flights is to become familiar with glider (high L/D) flying while observing and practicing energy management. All students will receive at least two glider flights.

CF 6215F.description: Time, weather, and proficiency allowing, select pilots will complete a familiarization and solo flight profile.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — T-38 ground school

CF 6301G.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6302A, CF 6303S, CF 6304S, CF 6305S under a shared description block.
CF 6301G.description: The T-38 ground school will include an introduction to the T-38 check flight phase and instruction on T-38 systems to include electrical abnormalities, avionics, engines, flight controls, and hydraulics. Training covers safety equipment and egress procedures using either a training device or aircraft. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with T-38 mission preparation, aircraft procedures, operations limits, and emergency procedures. Training occurs at the aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — T-38 egress academics

CF 6302A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6301G, CF 6303S, CF 6304S, CF 6305S under a shared description block.
CF 6302A.description: The T-38 ground school will include an introduction to the T-38 check flight phase and instruction on T-38 systems to include electrical abnormalities, avionics, engines, flight controls, and hydraulics. Training covers safety equipment and egress procedures using either a training device or aircraft. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with T-38 mission preparation, aircraft procedures, operations limits, and emergency procedures. Training occurs at the aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — T-38 egress training

CF 6303S.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6301G, CF 6302A, CF 6304S, CF 6305S under a shared description block.
CF 6303S.description: The T-38 ground school will include an introduction to the T-38 check flight phase and instruction on T-38 systems to include electrical abnormalities, avionics, engines, flight controls, and hydraulics. Training covers safety equipment and egress procedures using either a training device or aircraft. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with T-38 mission preparation, aircraft procedures, operations limits, and emergency procedures. Training occurs at the aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, FTE, RPA, and STC students — T-38 cockpit familiarization (non-pilot variant paired with CF 6305S)

CF 6304S.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6301G, CF 6302A, CF 6303S, CF 6305S under a shared description block. NRA variant of CF 6305S per Rule L.
CF 6304S.description: The T-38 ground school will include an introduction to the T-38 check flight phase and instruction on T-38 systems to include electrical abnormalities, avionics, engines, flight controls, and hydraulics. Training covers safety equipment and egress procedures using either a training device or aircraft. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with T-38 mission preparation, aircraft procedures, operations limits, and emergency procedures. Training occurs at the aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots — T-38 cockpit procedures (pilot variant paired with CF 6304S); per Rule J, T-38 applies to all pilots regardless of DG

CF 6305S.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6301G, CF 6302A, CF 6303S, CF 6304S under a shared description block. Pilot variant of CF 6304S per Rule L.
CF 6305S.description: The T-38 ground school will include an introduction to the T-38 check flight phase and instruction on T-38 systems to include electrical abnormalities, avionics, engines, flight controls, and hydraulics. Training covers safety equipment and egress procedures using either a training device or aircraft. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with T-38 mission preparation, aircraft procedures, operations limits, and emergency procedures. Training occurs at the aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: FTC — all standard (fixed-wing) students

CF 6310B.description: A complement to the T-38 Ground School, the self-paced Computer Based Training is to be accomplished in the student's own time. Topics include a systems overview, electrical, controls and displays, fuel system, air conditioning and pressurization.

applicabilityDetail.description: FTC — all standard (fixed-wing) students

CF 6320A.description: Training on the procedures and techniques necessary to reduce the probability of a compressor stall and flameout while operating the T-38.

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots — per Rule J, T-38 applies to all pilots regardless of DG

CF 6330E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6340E, CF 6341E, CF 6350E, CF 6351E under a shared description block.
CF 6330E.description: This testing includes the standard testing required in the AFI 11-2ftv2.

applicabilityDetail.description: CSO, P, and RPA students — T-38 closed book exam (paired with CF 6341E NRA variant per Rule L)

CF 6340E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6330E, CF 6341E, CF 6350E, CF 6351E under a shared description block. Paired with CF 6341E (NRA variant) per Rule L.
CF 6340E.description: This testing includes the standard testing required in the AFI 11-2ftv2.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM and FTE (Non-Rated Aircrew) — T-38 closed book exam (paired with CF 6340E Pilot variant per Rule L)

CF 6341E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6330E, CF 6340E, CF 6350E, CF 6351E under a shared description block. NRA = Non-Rated Aircrew. Paired with CF 6340E (pilot variant) per Rule L.
CF 6341E.description: This testing includes the standard testing required in the AFI 11-2ftv2.

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots — per Rule J, T-38 applies to all pilots regardless of DG

CF 6350E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6330E, CF 6340E, CF 6341E, CF 6351E under a shared description block. Paired with CF 6351E (mission-support variant) per Rule L.
CF 6350E.description: This testing includes the standard testing required in the AFI 11-2ftv2.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, FTE, and RPA students — T-38 mission-support boldface (paired with CF 6350E pilot variant per Rule L)

CF 6351E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6330E, CF 6340E, CF 6341E, CF 6350E under a shared description block. Paired with CF 6350E (pilot variant) per Rule L.
CF 6351E.description: This testing includes the standard testing required in the AFI 11-2ftv2.

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots — per Rule J, T-38 applies to all pilots regardless of DG. T-38 CF-1 of 3.

CF 6360F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6360-2F T-38 CF-1-3 (P)'. Per Rule G (tight-serial): position 1 retains full foundational prereq list.
CF 6360F.description: The objective of the T-38 check flights is to familiarize the student with T-38 aircraft operating procedures, AFTC local operations procedures, and primary divert base procedures. TPS students that meet the requirements of these blocks will be certified "crew solo".

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots — per Rule J, T-38 applies to all pilots regardless of DG. T-38 CF-2 of 3.

CF 6361F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6360-2F T-38 CF-1-3 (P)'. Per Rule G (tight-serial): position N>1 inherits full prereq list from MCG; graph-build may collapse to predecessor CF 6360F.
CF 6361F.description: The objective of the T-38 check flights is to familiarize the student with T-38 aircraft operating procedures, AFTC local operations procedures, and primary divert base procedures. TPS students that meet the requirements of these blocks will be certified "crew solo".

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots — per Rule J, T-38 applies to all pilots regardless of DG. T-38 CF-3 of 3.

CF 6362F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6360-2F T-38 CF-1-3 (P)'. Per Rule G (tight-serial): position N>1 inherits full prereq list from MCG; graph-build may collapse to predecessor CF 6361F.
CF 6362F.description: The objective of the T-38 check flights is to familiarize the student with T-38 aircraft operating procedures, AFTC local operations procedures, and primary divert base procedures. TPS students that meet the requirements of these blocks will be certified "crew solo".

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, and FTE students — T-38 aggregate flight training (non-pilot non-RPA variant per Rule L)

CF 6370F.eventNotes: Per Rule L, this is the non-pilot non-RPA T-38 variant alongside CF 6362F (pilots) and CF 6804F (RPA).
CF 6370F.description: The objective of the T-38 training flight is to introduce students to T-38 procedures, system operations, and checklist compliance. Students will become familiar with local flight and training operations as well apply basic aerodynamic principles during their sortie.

CF 6401G.eventName: Calspan Variable Stability System Learjet Ground School (ABM/CSO/RPA)
CF 6401G.moduleName: Calspan Variable Stability System Learjet Training

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, and RPA students — LJ-25 VSS ground school

CF 6401G.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6402-3F under a shared description block.
CF 6401G.description: Introduces Calspan's Variable Stability System Learjet. The ground school will cover basic operating procedures, limits, and safety/egress. The objective of the Learjet check flights is to provide working knowledge of the Learjet Ground Control Station. This training is for students in the Learjet data group and will consist of two flights operated from the ground control station.

CF 6402F.moduleName: Calspan Variable Stability System Learjet Training

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, and RPA students — LJ-25 CF-1 of 2

CF 6402F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6402-3F Learjet CF-1-2 (ABM/CSO/RPA)'. Grouped in MCG with CF 6401G under a shared description block.
CF 6402F.description: Introduces Calspan's Variable Stability System Learjet. The ground school will cover basic operating procedures, limits, and safety/egress. The objective of the Learjet check flights is to provide working knowledge of the Learjet Ground Control Station. This training is for students in the Learjet data group and will consist of two flights operated from the ground control station.

CF 6403F.moduleName: Calspan Variable Stability System Learjet Training

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, and RPA students — LJ-25 CF-2 of 2

CF 6403F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6402-3F Learjet CF-1-2 (ABM/CSO/RPA)'. Grouped in MCG with CF 6401G under a shared description block.
CF 6403F.description: Introduces Calspan's Variable Stability System Learjet. The ground school will cover basic operating procedures, limits, and safety/egress. The objective of the Learjet check flights is to provide working knowledge of the Learjet Ground Control Station. This training is for students in the Learjet data group and will consist of two flights operated from the ground control station.

CF 6410F.moduleName: Calspan Variable Stability System Learjet Training

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, and RPA students — onboard LJ-25 orientation

CF 6410F.description: Each student will also fly one flight onboard the Calspan Learjet for orientation with the VSS system. This can be completed in conjunction with CF 6402-3F.

prerequisites.name: Calspan Variable Stability System Learjet Ground School (ABM/CSO/RPA)

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — C-12 ground school

CF 6501G.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6502S, CF 6503S, CF 6504S, CF 6505S under a shared description block.
CF 6501G.description: Classroom instruction on the operation of major sub-systems in the C-12, basic emergency procedures, crew coordination responsibilities in the aircraft, and an introduction to C-12 performance takeoff data. Egress training covers safety equipment and egress procedures. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with C-12 mission at the aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students — C-12 egress training

CF 6502S.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6501G, CF 6503S, CF 6504S, CF 6505S under a shared description block.
CF 6502S.description: Classroom instruction on the operation of major sub-systems in the C-12, basic emergency procedures, crew coordination responsibilities in the aircraft, and an introduction to C-12 performance takeoff data. Egress training covers safety equipment and egress procedures. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with C-12 mission at the aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, FTE, and STC students — C-12 cockpit familiarization (non-pilot variant)

CF 6503S.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6501G, CF 6502S, CF 6504S, CF 6505S under a shared description block. Role-specific variant per Rule L alongside CF 6504S (CSO/RPA) and CF 6505S (P).
CF 6503S.description: Classroom instruction on the operation of major sub-systems in the C-12, basic emergency procedures, crew coordination responsibilities in the aircraft, and an introduction to C-12 performance takeoff data. Egress training covers safety equipment and egress procedures. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with C-12 mission at the aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: CSO and RPA students — C-12 cockpit procedures (CSO/RPA variant)

CF 6504S.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6501G, CF 6502S, CF 6503S, CF 6505S under a shared description block. Role-specific variant per Rule L alongside CF 6503S (ABM/FTE/STC) and CF 6505S (P).
CF 6504S.description: Classroom instruction on the operation of major sub-systems in the C-12, basic emergency procedures, crew coordination responsibilities in the aircraft, and an introduction to C-12 performance takeoff data. Egress training covers safety equipment and egress procedures. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with C-12 mission at the aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots — C-12 cockpit procedures (pilot variant)

CF 6505S.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6501G, CF 6502S, CF 6503S, CF 6504S under a shared description block. Role-specific variant per Rule L alongside CF 6503S (ABM/FTE/STC) and CF 6504S (CSO/RPA).
CF 6505S.description: Classroom instruction on the operation of major sub-systems in the C-12, basic emergency procedures, crew coordination responsibilities in the aircraft, and an introduction to C-12 performance takeoff data. Egress training covers safety equipment and egress procedures. The purpose of cockpit familiarization and procedures training is to familiarize the student with C-12 mission at the aircraft.

CF 6510E.eventName: C-12 Open Book Exam (P, crew solo)

applicabilityDetail.description: C-12 DG pilots only (crew solo)

CF 6510E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6520E, CF 6521E, CF 6530E under a shared description block.
CF 6510E.description: Testing conducted concurrently with T-38 testing.

CF 6520E.eventName: C-12 Pilot Closed Book Exam (P, crew solo)

applicabilityDetail.description: C-12 DG pilots only (crew solo)

CF 6520E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6510E, CF 6521E, CF 6530E under a shared description block. Paired with CF 6521E (MA / non-crew-solo variant) per Rule L.
CF 6520E.description: Testing conducted concurrently with T-38 testing.

CF 6521E.eventName: C-12 MA Closed Book Exam (ABM/CSO/FTE/P, non-crew solo/RPA)

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, FTE, non-C-12-DG pilots, and RPA — C-12 MA (Mission Aircrew) Closed Book Exam. Note: the (P, non-crew solo) qualifier only applies to the P audience; other audiences take the exam unconditionally.

CF 6521E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6510E, CF 6520E, CF 6530E under a shared description block. MA = Mission Aircrew. Paired with CF 6520E (pilot crew-solo variant) per Rule L.
CF 6521E.description: Testing conducted concurrently with T-38 testing.

applicabilityDetail.description: CSO, P, and RPA students — C-12 boldface. Despite 'Pilot' in the event name, per Rule F this does NOT imply pilot-only applicability.

CF 6530E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6510E, CF 6520E, CF 6521E under a shared description block. Per Rule F, 'Pilot' in the event name does not constrain applicability — the (CSO/P/RPA) tag controls.
CF 6530E.description: Testing conducted concurrently with T-38 testing.

applicabilityDetail.description: C-12 DG pilots only (crew solo) — C-12 CF-1 of 3

CF 6540F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6540-2F C-12 CF-1-3 (P, crew solo)'. Per Rule G (tight-serial): position 1 retains full foundational prereq list.
CF 6540F.description: The purpose of the C-12 check flights is to familiarize the student with C-12 aircraft operating procedures, local orientation of R-2515, and an orientation of the Edwards and Palmdale patterns. These blocks include three sorties for pilots only in the C-12 data group. TPS students that complete the blocks will be certified "crew solo" in the C-12.

prerequisites.name: C-12 Open Book Exam (P, crew solo)
C-12 Pilot Closed Book Exam (P, crew solo)

applicabilityDetail.description: C-12 DG pilots only (crew solo) — C-12 CF-2 of 3

CF 6541F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6540-2F C-12 CF-1-3 (P, crew solo)'. Per Rule G (tight-serial): position N>1 inherits full prereq list from MCG; graph-build may collapse to predecessor CF 6540F.
CF 6541F.description: The purpose of the C-12 check flights is to familiarize the student with C-12 aircraft operating procedures, local orientation of R-2515, and an orientation of the Edwards and Palmdale patterns. These blocks include three sorties for pilots only in the C-12 data group. TPS students that complete the blocks will be certified "crew solo" in the C-12.

CF 6550F.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6551F under a shared description block. Paired with CF 6551F (non-crew-solo pilots) per Rule L.
CF 6550F.description: The objective of the C-12 training flights is to introduce students to C-12 procedures, system operations, and checklist compliance. Students will become familiar with local flight and training operations as well as learn to calculate basic aircraft TOLD.

applicabilityDetail.description: Non-C-12-DG pilots (non-crew solo) — C-12 familiarization flight (complement to CF 6542F crew-solo)

CF 6551F.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6550F under a shared description block. Per Rule E, mutually exclusive with CF 6542F crew-solo per-pilot.

prerequisites.name: C-12 MA Closed Book Exam (ABM/CSO/FTE/P, non-crew solo/RPA)

applicabilityDetail.description: FTC — all standard (fixed-wing) students — F-16 ground school

CF 6601G.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6602A, CF 6603S, CF 6604S, CF 6605S under a shared description block.
CF 6601G.description: Classroom instruction introducing the F-16 check flight training requirements, a systems overview, and a detailed look at normal and emergency procedures. The academics also provide control room interaction training requirements, control room role in systems monitoring, and how the control room interacts during emergency procedures. Egress covers instruction on safety equipment and egress procedures. On aircraft/trainer training covering safety equipment and egress procedures.

applicabilityDetail.description: FTC — all standard (fixed-wing) students — F-16 egress academics

CF 6602A.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6601G, CF 6603S, CF 6604S, CF 6605S under a shared description block.

CF 6604S.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6601G, CF 6602A, CF 6603S, CF 6605S under a shared description block. Role-specific variant per Rule L alongside CF 6605S (P).
CF 6604S.description: Classroom instruction introducing the F-16 check flight training requirements, a systems overview, and a detailed look at normal and emergency procedures. The academics also provide control room interaction training requirements, control room role in systems monitoring, and how the control room interacts during emergency procedures. Egress covers instruction on safety equipment and egress procedures. On aircraft/trainer training covering safety equipment and egress procedures.

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots — F-16 cockpit procedures (pilot variant paired with CF 6604S)

CF 6605S.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6601G, CF 6602A, CF 6603S, CF 6604S under a shared description block. Role-specific variant per Rule L alongside CF 6604S (non-pilot).
CF 6605S.description: Classroom instruction introducing the F-16 check flight training requirements, a systems overview, and a detailed look at normal and emergency procedures. The academics also provide control room interaction training requirements, control room role in systems monitoring, and how the control room interacts during emergency procedures. applicabilityDetail.description: FTC — all standard students; executed by pilots, observed by others

CF 6610S.description: This simulator is executed by pilot students to practice emergency procedures but will be observed by all other students for awareness.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

CF 6620B.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6621C and CF 6622C under a shared description block.
CF 6620B.description: The asynchronous block introduces students to basic control room operations and presents the IADS® analysis software to the students. In addition to providing principles for good control room screen design, the block introduces the major capabilities of the IADS software as well as techniques to confirm proper system operation prior to mission start. Students will learn how to create real-time analysis windows in IADS, how to display data effectively, how to insert event markers and use test points to take notes, and how to use inherent IADS capabilities to streamline real-time execution. The objective of the Control Room Familiarization is to introduce the student to control room operations in a mission playback environment. The control room observation can be conducted along with any other curriculum control room event using live aircraft.

applicabilityDetail.description: All students

CF 6621C.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6620B and CF 6622C under a shared description block.
CF 6621C.description: The asynchronous block introduces students to basic control room operations and presents the IADS® analysis software to the students. Students will learn how to create real-time analysis windows in IADS, how to display data effectively, how to insert event markers and use test points to take notes, and how to use inherent IADS capabilities to streamline real-time execution. The objective of the Control Room Familiarization is to introduce the student to control room operations in a mission playback environment. The control room observation can be conducted along with any other curriculum control room event using live aircraft.

The objective of the Control Room Familiarization is to introduce the student to control room operations in a mission playback environment. The control room observation can be conducted along with any other curriculum control room event using live aircraft.

CF 6641E.eventName: F-16 MA Closed Book Exam (ABM/CSO/FTE/P, non-crew solo/RPA)

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, FTE, non-F-16-DG pilots, and RPA — F-16 MA (Mission Aircrew) Closed Book Exam. The (P, non-crew solo) qualifier only applies to the P audience.

CF 6641E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6630E, CF 6640E, CF 6650E, CF 6651E under a shared description block. MA = Mission Aircrew. Paired with CF 6640E (crew-solo pilot variant) per Rule L.
CF 6641E.description: Testing conducted concurrently with T-38 closed book testing.

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots (TPS student only) — F-16 CAPS exam

CF 6650E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6630E, CF 6640E, CF 6641E, CF 6651E under a shared description block. Paired with CF 6651E (WSO & MSF variant) per Rule L.
CF 6650E.description: Testing conducted concurrently with T-38 closed book testing.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, FTE, and RPA students — F-16 WSO (Weapon Systems Officer) & MSF (Mission Support Function) CAPS

CF 6651E.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6630E, CF 6640E, CF 6641E, CF 6650E under a shared description block. Paired with CF 6650E (pilot variant) per Rule L.
CF 6651E.description: Testing conducted concurrently with T-38 closed book testing.

CF 6660S.eventName: F-16 Emergency Procedures Checkout (P, crew solo)

applicabilityDetail.description: F-16 DG pilots only (crew solo) — Final EP checkout prior to last CF flight

CF 6660S.eventNotes: MCG lists CF 6672F as the sole prereq — unusual because CF 6660S is itself listed as a prereq of CF 6670-3F. Likely MCG intent: CF 6660S sits between CF 6672F (CF-3) and CF 6673F (CF-4); preserved verbatim.
CF 6660S.description: This simulator ensures the student can adequately respond to emergency procedures during crew solo operations prior to their final CF flight.

applicabilityDetail.description: F-16 DG pilots only (crew solo) — F-16 CF-1 of 4

CF 6670F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6670-3F F-16 CF-1-4 (P, crew solo)'. Per Rule G (tight-serial): position 1 retains full foundational prereq list.
CF 6670F.description: The purpose of the F-16 check flights is to familiarize the student with F-16 aircraft operating procedures, handling qualities, maneuvers, simulated flameout patterns, and operational limitations. Students will also have a working knowledge of the local complex and airspace. The blocks include four sorties for F-16 data group pilots only. TPS students that meet the requirements on all four sorties will be certified "crew solo" in the F-16.

applicabilityDetail.description: ABM, CSO, FTE, and RPA students — F-16 aggregate flight training (non-pilot variant)

CF 6680F.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6681F under a shared description block. Paired with CF 6681F (non-crew-solo pilots) per Rule L.
CF 6680F.description: The objective of the F-16 training flight is to introduce non-pilot students to F-16 procedures, system operations, and checklist compliance. Students will become familiar with local flight and training operations as well as observe basic aerodynamic principles during their sortie. Non-data group pilots will receive the F-16 Training Flight to introduce F-16 procedures, systems operations, and checklist compliance. The purpose of the training flight for student pilots, not in the F-16 group, is to provide a limited scope orientation flight to help prepare them for future flights on one of the main curriculum platforms.

prerequisites.name: F-16 Emergency Procedures Simulator (FTC) [req'd for P]
F-16 MA Closed Book Exam (ABM/CSO/FTE/P, non-crew solo/RPA)

applicabilityDetail.description: Non-F-16-DG pilots (non-crew solo) — F-16 familiarization flight (complement to CF 6673F crew-solo)

CF 6681F.eventNotes: Grouped in MCG with CF 6680F under a shared description block. Per Rule E, mutually exclusive with CF 6673F crew-solo per-pilot.
CF 6681F.description: The objective of the F-16 training flight is to introduce non-pilot students to F-16 procedures, system operations, and checklist compliance. Students will become familiar with local flight and training operations as well as observe basic aerodynamic principles during their sortie. Non-data group pilots will receive the F-16 Training Flight to introduce F-16 procedures, systems operations, and checklist compliance. The purpose of the training flight for student pilots, not in the F-16 group, is to provide a limited scope orientation flight to help prepare them for future flights on one of the main curriculum platforms.

applicabilityDetail.description: All pilots — T-38 formation flight; per Rule J, T-38 applies to all pilots regardless of DG

CF 6701F.description: These events orient the students with formation flying and prepare them for curriculum events required chase and target support.

applicabilityDetail.description: RPA pilots only — C-172 CF-1 of 2

CF 6801F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6801-2F C-172 CF-1-2 (RPA)'. MCG description references 'CF 6110-4F' for task pool source.
CF 6801F.description: Due to their previous aviation experience, RPA pilot students will only receive 1-2 C-172 flights that consist of a compilation of the contact and instrument tasks from CF 6110-4F. Emphasis will be placed on improving manned aircraft piloting skills.

applicabilityDetail.description: RPA pilots only — C-172 CF-2 of 2

CF 6802F.eventNotes: MCG lists this as a range: 'CF 6801-2F C-172 CF-1-2 (RPA)'.
CF 6802F.description: Due to their previous aviation experience, RPA pilot students will only receive 1-2 C-172 flights that consist of a compilation of the contact and instrument tasks from CF 6110-4F. Emphasis will be placed on improving manned aircraft piloting skills.

applicabilityDetail.description: RPA pilots only — C-12 CF-3 single-ship

CF 6803F.eventNotes: Numbered CF-3 within the RPA branching sequence (CF-1→CF-3→CF-4→CF-5/CF-6) per knowledge base §13.2.
CF 6803F.description: Single ship C-12 flight designed as a progressive buildup in manned aircraft piloting skills. This flight is accomplished after the C-172 flight(s). Also, this flight introduces to the student C-12 flight operations.

prerequisites.name: C-12 MA Closed Book Exam (ABM/CSO/FTE/P, non-crew solo/RPA)

applicabilityDetail.description: RPA pilots only — T-38 CF-4 single-ship

CF 6804F.eventNotes: Numbered CF-4 within the RPA branching sequence; RPA-specific T-38 variant per Rule L.
CF 6804F.description: Single ship T-38 flight designed as a progressive buildup in manned aircraft piloting skills. This flight is accomplished after the CF-3 C-12 Training Flight. Also, this flight introduces the student to T-38 flight operations.

applicabilityDetail.description: RPA pilots only — C-12 CF-5 formation

CF 6805F.eventNotes: Numbered CF-5 within the RPA branching sequence.
CF 6805F.description: Formation C-12 flight designed as a progressive buildup in manned aircraft piloting skills. This flight is accomplished after CF-3 or CF-5.

applicabilityDetail.description: RPA pilots only — T-38 CF-6 formation

CF 6806F.eventNotes: Numbered CF-6 within the RPA branching sequence.
CF 6806F.description: Formation T-38 flight designed as a progressive buildup in manned aircraft piloting skills. This flight is accomplished after CF-4 and may be accomplished prior to CF-5.
